title: Advanced Dynamic Programming and State Compression · Structure Brief: Knapsack Variants
summary: Scale sequence alignment and constrained-selection models beyond naive tables. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 knapsack variants increment in an optimized DP with equivalence evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish zero-one, unbounded, subset-sum, and value-indexed knapsack states.
Model longest-common-subsequence and edit-distance choices with boundary rows and reconstruction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
